question_text: A sharp knife blade has an area of 0.5 cm² and exerts a force of 50 N on a tomato. Calculate the pressure exerted by the knife blade. Give your answer in N/cm².
explanation: Using the formula P = F/A, we divide the force (50 N) by the area (0.5 cm²) to get 100 N/cm². This shows why sharp knives are effective: the small contact area creates high pressure, allowing the blade to cut through easily.
